title: Parent phase-map shows shipped child as 'Planned'; T012 falsely marked complete

findingDetails.evidence: Parent phase-map row reads '| 004 | 004-residual-design-units/ | … | Planned |', but the child spec.md:53 declares Status 'Resolved' (completion_pct 90, code committed). tasks.md:82 marks T012 [x] claiming 'parent phase-map row 30 status reflected' — that claim is false against the actual parent file.
findingDetails.why: The parent map is the canonical aggregate-progress surface (spec.md:116) that /speckit:resume reads to list child statuses; reporting a shipped unit as not-started misleads resume/operator. A checked verification task whose claim contradicts the file is a traceability defect.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update parent 005 spec.md row 004 status from 'Planned' to 'Complete' (or 'In Progress' if Unit C follow-on is tracked open), then either keep T012 [x] truthfully or note the residual follow-on.

title: Child spec.md continuity frontmatter never reconciled past scaffold

findingDetails.evidence: spec.md frontmatter still has completion_pct: 0 (line 28), recent_action 'Populated spec/plan/tasks/impl-summary', next_safe_action 'design Unit A vector reconcile first' (line 18), while the same file's METADATA says Status 'Resolved' and impl-summary/tasks frontmatter say completion_pct 90. spec §3 also still quantifies Unit A as ~314 rows / 5 missing-success while tasks T004 + impl-summary record 91 rows actually reset.
findingDetails.why: The completion rule requires reconciling _memory.continuity and spec.md status; a direct reader of spec.md's machine block would conclude the packet is 0% done with Unit A unstarted. Low operational impact only because the resume ladder reads continuity from implementation-summary.md (ADR-004).
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile spec.md frontmatter (completion_pct, recent_action, next_safe_action) to the resolved state and update the §3 Unit A row counts to the implemented 91-row reality, or add a one-line 'implemented numbers differ' note.
